---
title: "About"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: assets/images/eli-niktab.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 14em
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/eniktab
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/eliniktab/
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:maniktab@gmail.com
toc: false
---

I have research experience in academic, policy making, clinical, and industrial settings, specializing in exploring multi-feature data — particularly omics — through *in silico*, *in vitro*, and *in vivo* methodologies.

My primary interests include Applied AI for Health, Stochastic Modeling of Heterogeneous Systems, Manifold Learning for Regulatory Networks, and Functional Genomics.

## Computational Notes

This site is where I work out technical material on long-read genome assembly, graph neural networks, deep learning on biological sequence data, and the bits of pure graph theory and tropical algebra that fall out of those problems.

Posts are written as long-form lab-notebook entries: every result has a source-verified algorithmic background, a stated hypothesis, an experiment ledger, and a "what we learned" section. The intent is to share the reasoning trail, not just the headline number.

Code, model weights, and reproducibility scripts referenced in posts live in their respective repositories under [github.com/eniktab](https://github.com/eniktab).
